summary: Justin Brennan teaches how to acquire 10-30 unit apartment buildings with minimal capital ($20k-$50k out of pocket) by leveraging OPM (other people's money), syndication structures, and broker relationships. He debunks limiting beliefs about capital requirements, expertise needs, and deal sourcing, demonstrating specific deal structures where investors bring 10% of equity but receive 40% ownership plus acquisition fees.
takeaways: Target 10-30 unit deals for first investment (not 50+ units) - mom and pop sellers/brokers are more receptive to new operators
Bring only 10% of equity to get 40% ownership plus 2-3% acquisition fee on purchase price

First deal example: $1.5M property with $100k renovations + $50k closing costs = $1.65M total; 70% LTV loan = $500k down payment, but investor brings only $50k (10%) and nets $30k acquisition fee within 1 week of close, reducing out-of-pocket to $20k
Dedicate 5-10 hours/week to find deals while maintaining day job
Find deals two ways only: direct-to-broker (80% of deals sell pre-market in 2-week window before listing online) or direct-to-owner (via software like RealEstateRadar.ai)
Online listings (LoopNet, CoStar, Craigslist) show only ~40% of market - the 'leftovers' that don't make financial sense
Call brokers directly after touring 3 comparable properties in target market to get on their pre-market deal distribution list
Good deals attract money - focus on deal quality first (debt second, equity third); if deal makes financial sense financially (15-18%+ annual returns), capital raises itself
Typical investor returns 15-18%+ per year on capital - this is threshold that attracts private money from stock market/401k alternatives
31-unit case study: $19k out-of-pocket grew to $590k profit over 5 years (32x return) through acquisition fee ($45k), asset management fees ($120k), and sale profits ($470k)

skill_candidates.change: Systematize deal underwriting against 15-18% annual return threshold for investor capital attraction
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin emphasizes 'If the deal makes financial sense, the money will chase you' and specifies 15-18%+ returns as the standard that attracts capital; Brian needs to rapidly qualify/disqualify deals to source efficiently

skill_candidates.change: Implement 3-property tour + broker list capture workflow in target Georgia/Tennessee/Alabama markets
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin teaches: tour 3 comparable properties represented by different brokers in market, meet them in person, then ask to get on pre-market deal distribution list where 80% of deals sell before websites; addresses Brian's deal sourcing drag

skill_candidates.change: Pre-qualify markets on population growth, job growth, median household income 'green lines' before broker outreach
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin's Kansas City example: 'Population growth, job growth, median household income was all in the good green lines' before calling brokers; efficient market selection reduces wasted broker relationship time

title: How to Break Into Apartment Investing with No Money & No Network
